key_insight: AA-ViT uses anatomy-aware transformer guidance to synthesize contrast-enhanced brain MRI from standard MRI while better preserving lesion and tissue boundaries.
clinical_relevance: This could improve tumour visibility when gadolinium contrast is not feasible, potentially reducing scan burden, cost, and contrast-agent risk.
method: Vision Transformer for CEMRI synthesis from T1, T2, and FLAIR inputs
Adds structural guidance to preserve anatomical and tumour boundaries
Adds frequency guidance to improve fine-detail realism
evidence_signal: On BraTS 2021, the model outperformed state-of-the-art methods on PSNR and SSIM, and three neuroradiologists plus one neurosurgeon gave a mean clinical score of 3.94/5 across 19 cases.
watch_item: The clinical validation is preliminary and limited to a small retrospective sample, so broader external testing and safety checks are still needed.
paper_title: AA-ViT: Anatomically Aware Vision Transformer with Structural and Frequency Guidance for Contrast Enhanced Brain MRI Synthesis
